title: Ficus gemina
intro: Ficus gemina, also known as the two-leaf fig, is a plant species of the Ficus genus, which is widely distributed across tropical and subtropical regions. The health benefits of Ficus gemina include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Ficus gemina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as geminol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ficus gemina include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being and treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ficus gemina is a member of the banyan fig family and is classified as a species within the genus Ficus. It belongs to the family Moraceae. The genus Ficus is part of the order Rosales, which is a larger group of flowering plants. The order Rosales includes multiple families, such as the Urticaceae and Cannabaceae. Within the order Rosales, the family Moraceae is a significant group of flowering plants.
